prompt: We're debating whether to create a dedicated platform team or keep infrastructure responsibilities spread across our 4 product squads. Right now each squad manages their own infra, which leads to duplicated work, inconsistent practices, and occasional incidents that affect everyone. But forming a platform team means pulling 2–3 strong engineers out of product squads, which will slow feature delivery. My CTO wants a proposal by next week. We're a 40-person eng org.

assertions.text: The memo includes a clear one-sentence decision statement naming what's being decided and by when
The memo lists explicit assumptions with confidence levels (High/Medium/Low)
The memo explicitly separates customer value from internal convenience (e.g., notes which option is better for end-users/product quality vs. easier for engineers)
The memo has a section naming specific unknowns (e.g., actual cost of incidents, whether platform team would become a bottleneck)
The memo gives a clear recommendation (not hedged into 'it depends' without stating conditions)
The memo includes a risk table with likelihood, severity, and mitigation columns
